title: Chapter Fifty: Blood in the Water
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

"This place will do."

The voice came from everywhere and nowhere. Deep resonance. Internal energy of extreme depth.

*Thud. Thud.*

Corpses in blue martial clothes flew in from the treeline. Namgung family warriors -- the ones who had been secretly guarding the perimeter. Dead.

Every sword in the Lotus Pavilion cleared its scabbard at once.

*Shing!*

The unified sound of steel. No one asked for identities. They were rising talents of the orthodox realm, and they had learned this lesson before they could walk.

Blood-red hair mixed with black. Chilling aura. Twenty figures materialized from the undergrowth like ghosts given flesh.

Blood Swordmasters.

They revealed blood-red spirit light as they fixed their gaze on the place where Yeon-shin stood. Twenty pairs of eyes. All on him.

*Ma Jin warned me. After revealing Demon Destroying Blue Steel Hand, I became their primary target.*

*It was true.*

The Blood Flame Sect had tracked him through technique-based martial arts. An evil sect whose essence of power lay in sorcery -- the longer he spent in the open martial world, the more easily they could find him. If the Gathering had been inside the Namgung compound, this would not have happened.

But it had not.

"Blood Flame Sect?"

"Insane."

"So many swordmasters... how many branches have gathered?"

Sighs and muttered curses. Even Hwangbo Myeong-rin's face had gone white. Lazy Flame Dragon -- acupoints still sealed -- wore a grim expression.

The river flowed beneath the pavilion, swallowing sunlight. Only the sound of water.

*This is good.*

Yeon-shin circulated true energy along the predetermined verse. Buddhist divine power rose through his body, gathering in his right hand. The Seventh Apostle had lost an eye to this martial art. He hoped they had brought the nullifying technique. If he could break it again, there would be something to gain.

The lead Blood Swordmaster stepped forward. More black hair than the others -- greater refinement. Greater power.

"In a busy city like Huizhou, there is nowhere our sect's eyes do not reach. You crawled in knowing there is a death warrant on you. The fools of the orthodox sects knew nothing. You are--"

"Then you must know this too."

Yeon-shin let his hands fall to his sides.

Blue-green energy gathered in both palms. A unique martial art that existed nowhere else in the world.

Demon Destroying Blue Steel Hand.

The energy spread over the water like mist burning off a lake at dawn -- sky-blue, luminous, vast. Accumulated from weeks of internal energy cultivation, greater than it had ever been.

*Woong!*

Without even swinging his hands, the wave of blue-green force pushed back the entire group's energy. Complete opposition. The Blood Swordmasters' spirit light buckled and retreated, as if the mere presence of his technique was anathema to their blood arts.

For a moment, no one moved.

Then the lead swordmaster smiled.

"Good," he said. "Very good. You are everything the report claimed."

He raised his hand. Twenty Blood Swordmasters drew their swords in unison.

*They came here to kill me. Not to test. To kill.*

This was the martial world. A place where inevitability and chance intertwined to create corpses without warning. Just because you drove away a third-rate sect yesterday did not mean a major sect would not visit tomorrow. Even the Namgung family, who had locked their gates, might have been playing in the palm of the Blood Flame Sect's hand all along.

Behind him, Yeon-shin felt the rising talents -- thirty-odd young martial artists from a dozen sects, some barely older than himself, some trembling with fear they could not fully hide. He thought of what it meant. If these heirs of orthodox sects were massacred here, every veteran master in the realm would be shaken. In the world of masters, where the slightest difference in composure mattered, grief could be weaponized.

An old method of evil sects.

*If they scatter and run, they die in ones and twos. If they stand together, some live.*

He did not turn around.

"Stay behind me," he said. His voice carried across the pavilion, calm and clear.

"Who are you to give orders--" Hwangbo Myeong-rin began.

"Stay behind me."

The Blood Swordmasters came.

Not all at once. Four first -- probing, circling, their blood-red swords weaving patterns that made the air hum. Yeon-shin stepped forward to meet them, and Demon Destroying Blue Steel Hand blazed.

*Crash!*

The first blade met his palm and shattered. Blue-green energy detonated against blood technique, and the swordmaster reeled back with a scream, his arm blackened from wrist to elbow. The remaining three adjusted instantly -- closing the gap, attacking from three angles.

Yeon-shin twisted. Northern Light cleared its scabbard.

Steel met steel. Sparks flew like fireflies over the water. He parried one blade, ducked a second, caught the third swordmaster's wrist with his free hand and sealed the acupoint with a burst of Buddhist divine power. The man crumpled.

*Two down. Eighteen remaining.*

Six more came.

Yeon-shin planted his feet. True energy surged through every meridian. The Eternal Blossom Fist's accumulation principle began to build -- each exchange feeding the next, power compounding upon power.

Northern Light sang. Not the controlled sword song of practice -- a raw, desperate tone that cut through the roar of clashing energy like a bell through storm.

Behind him, the rising talents found their courage. Wei Wu-ling of the Jangbaek Sect charged in on his right flank. Yu Hyeon, battered as he was, raised his Mount Hua blade and took the left.

"Orthodox martial artists!" Yu Hyeon shouted, blood running from the corner of his mouth. "Together!"

*Good. They are fighters.*

But the Blood Swordmasters were not orthodox rising talents. They were killers refined in blood technique, and they fought with the coordinated savagery of wolves.

A blade found Yeon-shin's ribs. Heaven Silkworm thread held for one precious instant before the edge bit through. Pain bloomed, hot and sudden. He spun away, sealing it with internal energy, and answered with a sweeping arc of Demon Destroying Blue Steel Hand that drove three swordmasters back in a wave of blue-green fire.

*Twelve still standing.*

A headache pulsed at his crown. Familiar. The pressure that had been building for months -- the weight of the condition he did not name, the countdown he could not stop. His vision blurred for half a heartbeat, then cleared.

*Not now.*

He buried the pain and pressed forward.

Northern Light and the Blue Steel Hand worked in tandem. Sword in the left, blazing palm in the right. Every strike built upon the last. The Eternal Blossom Fist's principle -- accumulation without limit -- turned each exchange into fuel. He was faster now than he had been thirty seconds ago. Stronger.

The swordmasters felt it. He could see it in their eyes -- the dawning recognition that this boy was not weakening as the fight went on.

He was growing.

"Focus on the blue-handed one!" the lead swordmaster roared. "All of you!"

*All.*

Twelve remaining Blood Swordmasters converged. Behind them, the defeated ones were already healing -- blood technique knitting wounds, regenerating what should not regenerate.

The rising talents fought valiantly. Shin So-bin's blade work was precise and economical. Hyeon Won-chang's fists cracked against blood-hardened bodies with the enthusiasm of a man who had waited too long for a proper fight. Lazy Flame Dragon, one arm still weakened from the sealed acupoints, fought with his off hand and laughed while doing it -- a terrible, delighted sound.

But Yeon-shin was the target.

*Twelve swords. All for me.*

He gathered everything he had. Moon Spirit Harmony Technique drew energy from the world around him -- from the water, the wind, the fading sunlight. Jeong Family Dynamic Technique anchored him to the earth. Fate Defying Scripture amplified every strand of power.

And Demon Destroying Blue Steel Hand blazed like a star held in a human fist.

*WOONG!*

The wave erupted outward. Not a strike -- a detonation. Blue-green energy rolled across the water in concentric rings, shattering the surface into a thousand glittering fragments. Blood Swordmasters stumbled. Three went down. Two more staggered into the lake.

But the lead swordmaster cut through the wave.

His blade trailed blood-red light -- a technique Yeon-shin had not seen before, crimson energy spiraling along the steel like living flame. The collision of energies -- blue-green against crimson -- sent a pillar of force skyward that shook the pavilion's foundations. Roof tiles cracked. The water beneath the pavilion erupted in a geyser that soaked everyone within twenty paces.

Yu Hyeon fell, his Mount Hua robes torn and bloody, his sword arm hanging limp. Wei Wu-ling fell beside him, clutching a wound across his chest. All around the pavilion, rising talents were being driven to their knees by the sheer pressure of clashing energies, their own spirit lights guttering like candles in a gale.

Yeon-shin did not fall.

He stood at the center. Northern Light humming. Blue-green fire wreathing both arms. Blood running from his ribs and from a cut above his left eye that he did not remember receiving.

*Nine swordmasters still standing. More rising. Regeneration.*

*I cannot kill them fast enough.*

The headache surged. His crown burned. For one terrible instant, his vision split in two -- the world fracturing along invisible seams -- before snapping back into focus.

*Not now. Not yet.*

The question formed without his permission, cold and clear.

*Can I survive this?*

He thought of the World Tree's fruit. Of the Divine Sword Squad Leader's seat. Of Ma Jin waiting to strike the Hwangbo family. Of all the plans that required him to walk out of this pavilion alive.

The lead swordmaster was smiling again. Patient. Content. The smile of a predator who has cornered something and has all the time in the world.

Then -- hoofbeats.

Distant. Growing louder. Not from one direction.

From three.

The lead swordmaster's smile faltered. He turned his head, just slightly, toward the southern road.

Yeon-shin felt it before he saw it. Energy waves. Immense. Multiple sources converging on Huizhou like rivers feeding a flood.

The Namgung family, emerging from behind their locked gates.

Dust clouds from the north. The Hwangbo family's outriders, drawn by the chaos.

And from the west -- a third presence. Darker. Heavier. Something that prickled against Yeon-shin's skin like the memory of a nightmare.

*More Blood Flame. Reinforcements. Or something worse.*

The lead swordmaster barked an order. The Blood Swordmasters tightened their formation -- not retreating, not advancing. Waiting.

All around the Lotus Pavilion, the rising talents of the orthodox realm pressed together, bloodied and breathing hard, their eyes wide with the understanding that this was not a skirmish.

This was the opening movement of something far larger.

Yeon-shin lowered Northern Light. The blue-green fire dimmed to embers around his fists. Blood dripped from his ribs into the water below the pavilion, spreading in dark threads across the surface.

He looked at the three horizons. Three forces converging. The Blood Flame Sect. The great families. Allies and enemies arriving in the same breath.

The pressure at his crown pulsed once. Twice.

*Four years.*

From across the water, the Lazy Flame Dragon watched him with clear, lucid eyes. No madness in them now. Only recognition.

Two boys born to die young. Standing in the middle of a war neither of them had chosen.

The hoofbeats grew louder. The water stilled.

And in the fading light, the blood in the river spread.
